author: Dr. Lizard
title: What defines MDS with biallelic TP53 inactivation in the WHO-5 classification system?
answer: WHO-5 requires two TP53 mutations or one mutation plus TP53 copy loss or copy-neutral LOH.
detail: WHO-5 does not specify a minimum variant allele frequency threshold for TP53 mutations in MDS-biTP53.
The patient must still meet basic MDS diagnostic criteria including presence of dysplasia for WHO-5 classification.
MDS-biTP53 applies to cases with 0-19% blasts; at 20% blasts it becomes AML without TP53 designation.
Biallelic TP53 occurs in 66-78% of all TP53-mutated MDS cases and predicts poor prognosis.
releaseNote: WHO-5 classification system requires two TP53 mutations or one mutation plus TP53 copy loss or copy-neutral LOH for MDS with biallelic TP53 inactivation, with a minimum of 0-19% blasts and meeting basic MDS diagnostic criteria.
